full_prompt: Design the payload bay of a UKRoC 2025 school / university rocket entry that carries one or two raw Grade A large chicken eggs (mass approximately 60 g each) from launch to a recovery touchdown, both eggs surviving uncracked. Target apogee window 800 ft AGL (244 m) +/- 25 ft, flight duration 41-44 s from launch to touchdown, vehicle total GLOW 650 g including the 60 g (or 120 g for two-egg class) payload and motor. The payload bay is a cylindrical section of 41 mm outer diameter, length 180 mm, wall thickness 1.6 mm, made of filament-wound GFRP (E 18 GPa, tensile 300 MPa, density 1900 kg/m^3). Inside the bay is a 3D-printed PLA egg carriage with conforming internal geometry for the eggs, plus closed-cell polyethylene foam padding (foam stiffness modeled as linear with E 2 MPa, limiting its role to cushioning). Motor is an Aerotech F-class solid, peak thrust 50 N, total impulse 70 N*s. Recovery via a 46 cm diameter 6-line parachute deploying at apogee; descent rate 4.6 m/s at sea level. Touchdown is onto grass field (modeled as 2 g impact deceleration equivalent, since foam + parachute moderate impact to ~45 g peak at the egg, which is below the typical 80 g failure threshold for raw eggs). Load cases: (LC1) peak motor thrust 50 N axial on payload bay lower bulkhead + gravity on 650 g vehicle; LC1 stress is primarily in compression; (LC2) parachute opening shock at apogee: 150 N at the upper eyelet, tension; (LC3) ground impact: 2 g equivalent static on full 650 g decelerating structure; the egg itself sees a higher internal peak due to foam, but the bay wall only feels the 2 g average; (LC4) scrutineering / handling drop from 1 m onto concrete bench: 50 g equivalent static momentarily at the bay bottom edge; (LC5) integration stack weight: 500 g axial on top of the bay during assembly. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress under LC1-LC3 must remain below 150 MPa (300 / 2.0 FoS); (R2) under LC4 handling drop, stress must remain below 300 MPa (yield/ultimate, non-flight case); (R3) payload bay dry mass (GFRP shell + PLA egg carriage + foam) must not exceed 95 g to preserve GLOW budget; (R4) first natural frequency of bay with egg mass must exceed 100 Hz; (R5) eggshell impact acceleration under LC3 must remain below 45 g peak (this is an egg-level gate, verified via foam-element compression model - not a primary bay FEA gate but listed for completeness). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly (GFRP tube + PLA carriage + foam), FEA per load case, modal report, mass budget, and a note on egg-protection foam compression calculation.
short_prompt:
Design the payload bay of a UKRoC 2025 school/university rocket entry carrying one or two raw Grade A chicken eggs (60 g each) uncracked from launch to touchdown. Target apogee 800 ft AGL (244 m) +/- 25 ft, flight duration 41-44 s, GLOW 650 g including payload and motor (Aerotech F-class, 50 N peak, 70 N-s impulse). 46 cm 6-line parachute at apogee, 4.6 m/s descent to grass.

Envelope: filament-wound GFRP bay tube, 41 mm OD x 180 mm long x 1.6 mm wall; 3D-printed PLA egg carriage conforming to the egg; closed-cell polyethylene foam (E = 2 MPa, cushion-only).

Factor regime: GFRP material FoS 2.0 for flight cases (allowable 150 MPa); scrutineering drop allowed to full yield/ultimate; dry mass (GFRP + PLA carriage + foam) <= 95 g; loaded first frequency > 100 Hz; egg peak accel gate 45 g (egg-level, foam-compression calc).

Load cases.
- Peak motor thrust 50 N axial on the lower bulkhead + gravity on 650 g vehicle.
- Parachute opening shock: 150 N at the upper eyelet tension.
- Ground impact at 4.6 m/s: 2 g equivalent static on 650 g (bay-level; egg peak ~45 g is separate).
- Bench drop from 1 m onto concrete: 50 g equivalent static at the bay bottom edge.
- Integration stack weight: 500 g axial on top of the bay.

prompt.title: UKRoC 2025 Egg-Payload Rocket Bay
prompt.brief: GFRP tube payload bay with PLA carriage + foam for egg transport, 800 ft apogee, 41-44 s duration, 46 cm parachute.

mission.motor: Aerotech F-class, 50 N peak, 70 Ns total

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for GFRP tube, TET10 for PLA carriage, solid for foam block (linear E)

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: UKRoC current-year rulebook PDF not scraped; envelope and apogee/duration taken from UKRoC public brief + the US student rocketry benchmark equivalent (UKRoC is the UK sister event).

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Parachute descent reduced to shock load LC2.
